title: Quieting Food Noise After GLP-1 Discontinuation: A Feasibility Study of a Novel Mindful Eating Program
authors_full: Swan¹, PD, PhD, FACSM, FTOS, Associate Professor; May², ML, MD, Faculty Associate Professor; Howell³, LA, PhD, ABPP, Clinical Health Division Chair; Lincenberg⁴, DL, Consultant, PsyD, LP, ABPP

summary.hook: This poster proposes and tests the feasibility of a 12-week online mindful eating program as a structured “off-ramp” for adults who discontinue GLP-1 receptor agonist therapy.
summary.summary_30s: This poster proposes and tests the feasibility of a 12-week online mindful eating program as a structured “off-ramp” for adults who discontinue GLP-1 receptor agonist therapy.

key_numbers: Intervention duration: 12 weeks (12 weekly sessions; 90 minutes each).
Adults who discontinued GLP-1 receptor agonist therapy within 2–3 weeks (no diabetes; no active eating disorder...
Poster reports background statistics: 12% of adults have taken a GLP-1; 54% discontinue by 1 year; 72% within 2 years...
This poster proposes and tests the feasibility of a 12-week online mindful eating program as a structured “off-ramp” for...
takeaways: This poster is primarily a protocol/rationale poster; empirical outcome results are Not stated on poster.
The poster provides background statistics suggesting high GLP-1 uptake, high discontinuation, frequent re-initiation, and substantial weight regain after stopping...
The poster proposes a conceptual model (“Chaos → Calm Adaptive Pathway Model”) in which discontinuation creates a vulnerability window; mindful eating is framed as...

researcher: How do you define “food noise” in this study, and how does the RAID-FN Food Noise Inventory capture it?
